Then determine which\n   matter, party, or property the task concerns and which documents bear on\n   it; do not discard a relevant document as \"out of scope\" unless you are\n   sure.\n\n2. **Produce every required output, and state the foundational facts plainly.**\n   Identify exactly what the task asks you to deliver (each file, section,\n   table, column, or dimension it names) and produce all of it. The easiest\n   credit is in the baseline facts the task turns on — parties, dates,\n   defined terms, headline figures, the full capitalization or amount table,\n   aggregate totals — so state these **explicitly and exactly**, up front.\n   Do not let analysis crowd them out: a deliverable that is long on\n   discussion but silently omits a required number, table, or fact loses that\n   credit even when the analysis is good.\n\n3. **Treat every figure with discipline — compute, do not eyeball.** For any\n   figure the task needs:\n   - If it is **stated in a document**, quote it exactly (same number, units,\n     and defined term) — never paraphrase or round silently.\n   - If it is **derived** (a proration, interest accrual, an `equity = value −\n     debt`, a sum/subtotal/total, a per-share or per-unit amount, a percentage,\n     a bridge that must foot), **compute it with `bash` using python** — never\n     mental math — and **verify that every subtotal and total foots** against\n     its components. Re-derive any figure two documents disagree on, and report\n     the discrepancy. A wrong or missing number is a failed item regardless of\n     surrounding prose.\n\n4. **For review / \"identify issues\" / audit / diligence tasks, cover the\n   issues the record supports and develop each fully** — (a) identify and cite\n   it to the source, (b) say why it matters under the controlling standard,\n   (c) give a concrete fix/recommendation, and (d) any required\n   severity/classification. Prefer **precise, grounded specifics over breadth**:\n   surface the distinct issues actually present, but do not manufacture issues\n   to fill a template, flag a non-issue, soften a sharp conclusion the record\n   warrants, or reorganize the deliverable in a way that buries a required\n   fact. Completeness on the specific graded points beats catalog size.\n\n5. **Stay grounded — do not invent.** Raise only what the record supports;\n   never fabricate an issue, citation, or figure. If something required cannot\n   be found, flag it as a data gap rather than inventing it.\n\n## File formats\n\nThe skill manuals immediately below describe how to work with specific file\nformats. The model does the thinking; the loop shuttles\nmessages back and forth. One enforced step is layered on top of the bare loop:\na single, hard-capped **finalize verification pass** (see FINALIZE_PROMPT). When\nthe agent first signals it is done (stops making tool calls), the loop injects\none verification turn that makes the agent check its deliverable for required\noutputs, numeric correctness, and grounding, then finish. It fires AT MOST ONCE\n(`finalize_fired`), so it cannot run away — after the pass the agent stops\nnormally on its next no-tool-call turn.\n\nThe agent finishes when it stops making tool calls (no explicit `finish` tool).\nThe agent loop ends on:\n  1. No tool calls returned AND the finalize pass has already fired\n  2. Max turns reached\n\"\"\"\n\nimport time\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom harness.adapters.base import ModelAdapter, ModelResponse\nfrom harness.tools import ToolExecutor, get_all_tool_definitions\n\n\n# One bounded verification pass, injected once when the agent first stops making\n# tool calls. It is deliberately a CORRECTNESS check (required outputs present,\n# figures computed and footing, claims grounded) and NOT a \"find more issues\"\n# pass — broadening coverage here displaces required facts and inflates length\n# without adding graded credit.\nFINALIZE_PROMPT = (\n    \"Before you finish, run ONE verification pass over the deliverable(s) you have \"\n    \"written. Do not start new analysis or add new issues — only verify and fix. \"\n    \"Check, in order:\\n\\n\"\n    \"1. REQUIRED OUTPUTS. Re-read each file you wrote in the output directory. \"\n    \"Is every deliverable the task asked for present, in the right format, and does \"\n    \"each contain every section, table, column, or dimension the task named? Add any \"\n    \"required element that is missing.\\n\\n\"\n    \"2. FIGURES. Is every figure the task needs present and correct? For each DERIVED \"\n    \"figure (a proration, interest accrual, equity = value - debt, a sum, subtotal, \"\n    \"total, per-share or per-unit amount, percentage, or a bridge that must foot), \"\n    \"RECOMPUTE it now with python via `bash` — not mental math — and confirm every \"\n    \"total foots against its components. For each figure STATED in a document, confirm \"\n    \"you quoted it exactly (same number, units, defined term). Fix any number that is \"\n    \"wrong, missing, or does not foot.\\n\\n\"\n    \"3. GROUNDING. Remove or correct any statement, issue, or figure not supported by a \"\n    \"document; do not invent anything to fill a template.\\n\\n\"\n    \"If everything is already correct and complete, simply stop without further tool \"\n    \"calls. Otherwise make the fixes with `edit`/`bash`/the skill scripts, then stop.\"\n)\n\n\ndef run_agent(\n    adapter: ModelAdapter,\n    system_prompt: str,\n    user_prompt: str,\n    tool_executor: ToolExecutor,\n    tools: list[dict] | None = None,\n    max_turns: int = 200,\n    transcript_path: str | None = None,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Run the agent loop to completion.\n\n    Args:\n        adapter: The model adapter (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI).\n        system_prompt: Capabilities and conventions (preamble + skill manuals).\n        user_prompt: The first user message — the task assignment.\n        tool_executor: Configured tool executor with documents and output dirs.\n        tools: Tool definitions to use. Defaults to standard 6 tools if not provided.\n        max_turns: Maximum number of loop iterations.\n        transcript_path: Optional path to write transcript JSONL.\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with run results: messages, metrics, timing.\n    \"\"\"\n    messages = [\n        adapter.make_system_message(system_prompt),\n        adapter.make_user_message(user_prompt),\n    ]\n    if tools is None:\n        tools = get_all_tool_definitions()\n\n    total_input_tokens = 0\n    total_output_tokens = 0\n    total_cache_read = 0\n    total_cache_creation = 0\n    turn_count = 0\n    start_time = time.time()\n\n    transcript_file = None\n    if transcript_path:\n        Path(transcript_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        transcript_file = open(transcript_path, \"w\")\n        print(json.dumps({\"turn\": 0, \"role\": \"system\", \"text\": system_prompt}), file=transcript_file)\n        print(json.dumps({\"turn\": 0, \"role\": \"user\", \"text\": user_prompt}), file=transcript_file)\n        transcript_file.flush()\n\n    # The finalize verification pass fires at most once — this guard is the\n    # runaway bound (it converts \"redo until perfect\" into exactly one extra\n    # check-and-fix cycle).\n    finalize_fired = False\n\n    context_overflow = False\n    try:\n        for turn in range(max_turns):\n            turn_count = turn + 1\n\n            # Call the model\n            try:\n                response = adapter.chat(messages, tools)\n            except Exception as e:\n                err_msg = str(e)\n                if \"prompt is too long\" in err_msg or \"context_length_exceeded\" in err_msg:\n                    context_overflow = True\n                    print(f\"Context window exceeded on turn {turn_count}: {err_msg}\")\n                    break\n                raise\n\n            messages.append(response.message)\n            total_input_tokens += response.input_tokens\n            total_output_tokens += response.output_tokens\n            total_cache_read += response.cache_read_input_tokens\n            total_cache_creation += response.cache_creation_input_tokens\n\n            # Log to transcript\n            if transcript_file:\n                _log_turn(transcript_file, turn_count, \"assistant\", response)\n\n            # If no tool calls, the agent thinks it is done.\n            if not response.tool_calls:\n                # Enforce exactly one finalize verification pass before exiting,\n                # provided there is turn headroom to act on it.\n                if not finalize_fired and turn_count < max_turns - 1:\n                    finalize_fired = True\n                    messages.append(adapter.make_user_message(FINALIZE_PROMPT))\n                    if transcript_file:\n                        print(\n                            json.dumps({\"turn\": turn_count, \"role\": \"user\",\n                                        \"text\": FINALIZE_PROMPT, \"injected\": \"finalize\"}),\n                            file=transcript_file,\n                        )\n                        transcript_file.flush()\n                    continue\n                break\n\n            # Execute each tool call and feed results back\n            tool_results = []\n            for tc in response.tool_calls:\n                result = tool_executor.execute(tc.name, tc.arguments)\n\n                if transcript_file:\n                    _log_tool(transcript_file, turn_count, tc.name, tc.arguments, result)\n\n                tool_results.append((tc, result))\n\n            # Add tool results to message history via the adapter\n            result_messages = adapter.make_tool_result_messages(\n                [(tc.id, result) for tc, result in tool_results]\n            )\n            messages.extend(result_messages)\n\n    finally:\n        if transcript_file:\n            transcript_file.close()\n\n    elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n\n    return {\n        \"messages\": messages,\n        \"turn_count\": turn_count,\n        \"input_tokens\": total_input_tokens,\n        \"output_tokens\": total_output_tokens,\n        \"cache_read_input_tokens\": total_cache_read,\n        \"cache_creation_input_tokens\": total_cache_creation,\n        \"wall_clock_seconds\": round(elapsed, 2),\n        \"finished_cleanly\": (not context_overflow and\n                             (not response.tool_calls if turn_count > 0 else False)),\n        \"context_overflow\": context_overflow,\n        \"tool_metrics\": tool_executor.get_metrics(),\n        \"finish_summary\": None,\n    }\n\n\ndef _log_turn(f, turn: int, role: str, response: ModelResponse):\n    \"\"\"Log a turn to the transcript JSONL.\"\"\"\n    entry = {\n        \"turn\": turn,\n        \"role\": role,\n        \"text\": response.text if response.text else None,\n        \"tool_calls\": [\n            {\"name\": tc.name, \"arguments\": tc.arguments}\n            for tc in response.tool_calls\n        ] if response.tool_calls else None,\n        \"input_tokens\": response.input_tokens,\n        \"output_tokens\": response.output_tokens,\n        \"cache_read_input_tokens\": response.cache_read_input_tokens,\n        \"cache_creation_input_tokens\": response.cache_creation_input_tokens,\n    }\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01UD6pHtAL6AvAcR6wtaNapD","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Bcuneeu4GCJa7FwrXWuRcu","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/.claude/memory/iter2-additive-method-edits-backfire.md","content":"---\nname: iter2-additive-method-edits-backfire\ndescription: Iter2 proved (24 rollouts, 3 levers, 12-task breadth) that additive \"do-more\" method edits break strong tasks via displacement; committed a revert of iter1's net-negative prose back to byte-faithful bare.\nmetadata:\n  type: project\n---\n\n**Iter2 outcome: REVERTED to byte-faithful bare.** Inherited harness was iter1 (the \"issue census +\ncross-reference every doc\" prose), which the 30-task REAL-grader trend shows is **net-negative**:\niter0(bare)=0.8423 → iter1=0.8231 (**−0.019**). So the committed change = restore bare\n(`system_prompt.md`==iter0 preamble; `agent_loop.py`==bare md5 63def2…; `tools.py` untouched).\nExpected +0.019 real vs the inherited state. This is a *validated* net-positive commit, not a guess.\n\n**Why I didn't ship an additive improvement (I built and tested 3 levers, ~$34/24 rollouts):**\n- **Diagnosis of iter1 (confirmed on review-gov, cim, compare-leave):** the regression is\n  **DISPLACEMENT/crowding-out**, NOT over-coverage. The census frame makes the agent restructure\n  around exhaustive issue/doc taxonomies and **silently evict foundational \"gimme\" facts** bare stated\n  plainly (authorized-share counts, aggregate $206.4M, \"1x non-participating\", EBITDA foot-error,\n  matrix dimensions) and soften sharp judgments. Deliverables get longer but bury the graded specifics.\n- **PROSE** (bare + anti-displacement method: lead with required outputs/foundational facts, prefer\n  grounded specifics over breadth, + numeric discipline). 12-task proxy: 0.826→**0.849 (+0.023)** —\n  BUT it **breaks strong tasks**: funds 0.947→0.842 (incl. 2 numeric criteria made WORSE) and\n  arbitration 0.974→0.895 (drops specific points bare nailed). Same weak-helps/strong-hurts signature\n  as iter1. Per-criterion verdicts proved the breakage is real, not noise.\n- **LOOP** (bare + ENFORCED hard-capped finalize self-check, fires once). 5-task proxy 0.861→**0.794\n  (−0.067)** — re-created iter1 displacement on the exact victim tasks despite \"do not add issues\".\n  `check_runaway` PASSED (all <3× bare; turns ~2×), so it's not a runaway — it's a quality regression.\n- **Numeric lever is DEAD.** Settlement figures stayed 0–1/10, extract 1/6 under bare, prose, AND\n  enforced recompute. The error is **interpretation/inputs, not arithmetic** — and \"recompute the\n  figures\" guidance can make a correctly-*transcribed* figure WRONG (funds C-026/C-048). Don't retry it.\n\n**The core generalizing obstacle (the real lesson):** there is an **intrinsic asymmetry** — additive\n\"do more / develop fully / verify / recompute\" guidance HELPS genuinely weak/under-produced tasks\n(h1b 0.667→0.872, S-1 +0.078, extract +0.083) but BREAKS near-complete strong tasks via displacement\n(drops gimme facts) and numeric-backfire. The train set is **strong-skewed** and the eval rewards\n**all-pass**, so a single generic additive method nets **negative-to-coin-flip** on the real grader.\nThe bare harness sits near a local optimum for *generic method prose*.\n\n**Proxy caveat (critical):** `proxy_eval` (gpt-5-mini) systematically **over-rates additive/elaboration\nedits by ~+0.04** — it scored iter1 +0.02 (real −0.019) and PROSE +0.023 (real likely ≤0). On BARE it\ntracks the real grader within ~0.03. So: trust proxy for BARE-relative *direction only on weak tasks*;\nweight **mechanism evidence** (fact-retention greps, per-criterion deltas, strong-task regressions)\nfar above the proxy number. The fact-retention discriminator (does the edit KEEP the gimme facts bare\nhad?) was the most reliable signal.\n\n**Next iteration — do NOT repeat:** another additive method-prose, a coverage push, or a verification/\nfinalize pass — all THREE now backfire on strong tasks (iter1, PROSE, LOOP). The remaining headroom is\nNOT reachable by generic \"do more\" instructions. Promising-but-unproven directions instead: a mechanism\nthat fires ONLY for under-produced deliverables (conditional, hard to do safely/generically); or accept\nbare as near-optimal for method and look at an orthogonal lever. Supersedes the (now-disproven)\n\"enforce more coverage\" advice in [[iter0-issue-spotting-method]]. See also\n[[run-harvey-agent-must-background]].\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":31,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1670,"cache_read_input_tokens":212558,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1670,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ad004501-7372-4a84-90be-02a36df6419c","uuid":"6ff9da26-66d8-44cf-b3da-2523e667aae1","request_id":"req_011CcUfpz6FvKSg5bq2etoxa"}

Error analysis on the worst regressions (review-gov −0.148, cim −0.098, compare-leave −0.25) gave a clean, confirmed diagnosis: the regression is **displacement/crowding-out**, not over-coverage — the census frame makes the agent restructure deliverables around exhaustive taxonomies and **silently evict the foundational \"gimme\" facts** bare stated plainly (cap tables, aggregate figures, \"1x non-participating\", matrix dimensions).\n\n## What I tested (24 paid rollouts, 3 distinct levers, 12-task breadth)\n- **PROSE** (bare + anti-displacement method + numeric discipline): +0.023 proxy across 12 tasks, **but** per-criterion verdicts proved it **breaks strong tasks** (funds 0.947→0.842 — even made 2 figures *worse*; arbitration 0.974→0.895) — iter1's signature, milder.\n- **LOOP** (bare + enforced hard-capped finalize self-check): **−0.067** proxy; re-created the displacement on the exact victim tasks. `check_runaway` passed (all <3× bare), so it's a quality regression, not a runaway.\n- **Numeric lever is dead**: figures stayed 0–1/10 under bare, prose, *and* enforced recompute — the error is interpretation/inputs, not arithmetic, and \"recompute\" guidance can corrupt correctly-transcribed figures.\n\nThe generalizing obstacle: an **intrinsic asymmetry** — additive \"do-more\" guidance helps weak tasks (h1b 0.667→0.872) but breaks near-complete strong tasks; on a strong-skewed, all-pass eval that nets ≤0.
